title: From DEM to TWI
description: Walk through every step from raw elevation data to a Topographic Wetness Index map — with a clickable 5×5 DEM, live slope calculator, interactive watershed map, and executable Python cells.
author: Mohammad Galib, Purdue University

citation.text: Beven, K.J. & Kirkby, M.J. (1979). A physically based, variable contributing area model of basin hydrology. Hydrological Sciences Bulletin, 24(1), 43–69.

title: Reading a Hydrograph
description: Scroll through the anatomy of a storm hydrograph — rising limb, peak, recession — then drag a Lyne–Hollick α slider to separate baseflow live, watch a 2-panel storm comparison expose non-linear runoff response, and run a Python cell that generates a CAMELS-style annual hydrograph for three contrasting gauges.
author: Mohammad Galib, Purdue University

citation.text: Lyne, V. & Hollick, M. (1979). Stochastic time-variable rainfall-runoff modelling. Inst. Eng. Australia Nat. Conf. Publ. 79/10, 89–93.
